channel.title: Comments for page "The Critic"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "The Critic"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
He could be.

Or he could, as his name implies, be a technician who's well-versed in the scientific side of his art and isn't shy about showing it off.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like this in a lot of senses, and while it might not fit my headcanon of AWCY? precisely, it probably comes the closest out of all the AWCY? tales I've read.
Something I did find kinda weird;

"It's produced by a memetic agent that specifically targets felis catus," the Director said. "They're compelled to come to this particular site and fight each other until they're fatally wounded, and to lie down before they die in patterns that spell out the messages."

is that the Director is apparently an undercover Foundation agent, who's doing a pretty bad job of staying undercover. I mean, this seems like a pretty strange way for an artist to talk.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is one of the only AWCY articles I like, aside from the fact that it doesn't fit my headcanon of them. Well, nothing on the site fits my headcanon of them, so I wouldn't dock it for that anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It takes place in Chelsea for a reason.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just got the 69th upvote on this. Ignoring the previous immaturity, it was an excellent tale. And the Critic is right; art should shock people and make them think outside the box, not do the same thing over and over.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
He never explicitly stated it was 804, neither was it stated in the original document that sparked this GOI. They refer to the same thing, but never force it to be 804.

Sorts commented on this earlier, by the way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is excellent, with one nitpick: it's kinda shitty to reference Sorts' SCP when he explicitly doesn't want to tie it to AWCY. Dick move. Upvoted, but you should remove that reference or recast it as an inspiration to AWCY rather than a creation of it (which would nicely match the real-life happenings).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This right here? This is what we call constructive criticism. Hopefully it will cut down on the sub-par posts tryign to hide behidn an AWCY? sticker. Although that would mean less angst and AWCY? bashing, which I do so enjoy…

I used to think of AWCY? as completly anarchistic, with no leadership other than the dominant personality in a given clique. Now, my head canon has been updated to include an "old guard" with de facto authority over the whole mob because all the little wannabes and art school drop-outs crave their approval. Not only is it a more interesting group dynamic, it's closer to the social structire I've seen emerge among "creatives" IRL.

+all the votes for putting out some valid criticism and a good story at the same time. And you didn't even have to resort to plagarism, I mean parody.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: AWCY? as meme is a better concept than any AWCY? scp to date.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Huh. Didn't know this existed.

So this is what the fellow was talking about when he mentioned that tale was similiar to SCP-1217. I don't know how to feel.

You'll notice that I upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
For what it's worth, its author had to explain several times that "No, that's not what I meant when I wrote that."

It just kind of seems … to disregard the wishes of an author on a shared creative wiki site, basically to that author's face.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For what it's worth, death of the author has been around a little longer than TvTropes.

item.title: No.
item.content:encoded: Because they wouldn't say "Durr hurr, it's art, it must be that one GoI."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I meant "even if" in the "Despite the fact" sense.

As to why they would assume that 804 was AWCY??
Of course something like 804 had to be their work. Who else could have made it? After all, they're the ones with their fingers on the pulse. They're the ones who actually know what's going on.

Basically, they're a bunch of twits who think they've actually got a handle on things. There might be one or two of them that actually have something worth saying, but those few voices are drowned out by idiots. In their own way, they're more arrogant than the Foundation could ever hope to be.

Actually, that's something I quite like about this story. For all that the Critic's views are similar to those expressed by the site, in universe, you could make a strong argument that he's just as full of shit as the people he's criticizing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yes.

Seriously though there was no clown winking and pointing at a neon sign that said the word COOL, how could anyone in AWCY? think they had a hand in it?

But you know what, that's okay because there's a page on TV Tropes somewhere that says Death Of The Author and that means that you can pretend that words mean anything you want, and that's just how AWCY would like it because Dada.

Here is a picture of a fish
lol art

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not entirely sure I understand what that statement means.
'Cause I'm reading it as either displeasure at the use of 'even if" in my statement implying that it may be an AWCY? piece in contradiction of your statements, as the author, to the contrary or a general displeasure at seeing your work referenced in conjunction with something you don't like.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Regarding 804: even if AWCY? had nothing to do with that, I bet there are plenty in the organization who claim (and maybe even believe) that it was the work of AWCY?

That's a pretty good reason to keep biting on my shit except not really.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, my headcanon regarding AWCY? is that it's an extremely factional group, with a few established cliques surrounded by a mass of angry young wannabes. The wannabes are simultaneously starving for recognition from the Cliques and resentful of them. The term "The Man" gets bandied about a lot
I imagine that the Critic belongs to one of the older cliques.
In this setup, most of the crap AWCY? are the products of the wannabes trying to win favor with/take down the various cliques. There are probably quite a few AWCY? pieces which are just jabs at a different cliques views.
I also imagine that there's a fringe group (the ones who don't have enough talent to even create a bad AWCY? installation), who just wander around and spray paint "Are We Cool Yet?" near weird shit they had nothing to do with.

Regarding 804: even if AWCY? had nothing to do with that, I bet there are plenty in the organization who claim (and maybe even believe) that it was the work of AWCY?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's the same thing my wife says when we argue. Well, not really. She just keeps yelling. But it's the thought that counts.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Maybe there is no "real" AWCY in the same way that there is no canon. The AWCY canon is dozens of hundreds of separate organizations, springing up and dying off month by month or week by week. The only thing that connects them is that their art is anomalous or related to anomalous items.

Maybe Are We Cool Yet is a memetic phenomenon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is it bad if I kinda hope Boah's version of AWCY? described above is met by the SPC instead of the Foundation?

I know they aren't sharks, but exceptions could be made I'm sure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was even stated in the tale that was only a sect of them. So he's not the only "authority figure" they have. Plus everything he said was spot on. So unless that stops we have no problems.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dammit Doktori, you keep your logic and reasoned common sense out of this!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm upvoting this because of the quality of your writing, and the excellent points you raise about art and the AWCY? movement.

There are some things that don't sit well with me though. Firstly, I'm not crazy about tying 804 in to AWCY?, since it predates them, and I honestly think that it works better without having any kind of connection to AWCY?. Tying it to the group removes that question of whether it is memetic or whether it was built intentionally which I think helps elevate that article a lot. If it was something unrelated that might have inspired some of AWCY?, I think that would be a bit better, but explicitly stating that it was built by them I think doesn't help this tale that much, but can lessen 804.

This also doesn't really mesh with my headcanon interpretation of AWCY?. I like thinking of them as a collection of groups with no real organizational structure who probably hate each other as much as they hate the Foundation, only some of whom are actually part of the AWCY? group as defined by the Foundation, the rest do things without feeling the need to attach their name to it. Some of the less interesting AWCY? articles, that just kill people and add their catchphrase at the end without much thought, I like to imagine are just imitators who want to be a part of the group. Basically, I don't like the way this feels too much like an actual hierarchy in AWCY?, which I don't think is appropriate for the group.

EDIT: I am now realizing that I was totally discounting the idea that this was only one cell of AWCY?, which would actually fit in quite well. I feel kind of silly for forgetting about that. I'm still not crazy about attributing all of the recent stuff the Foundation has found to one cell, but I am more ok with this story now.

Yeah, I know, I know, no canon and whatnot. Just my two cents.

That said, this is well written, and is a great way to express commentary on what's been going on on the site lately.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If recontextualization is the direction you want to go, talk to Clipper - that containment file he garbled all up with newspaper headlines and sent right back to the Man's front door was brilliant."

:D

To be clear, though, I'm not upvoting this because it speaks favorably of one of my works- I like it because, as TL333S said, this is how you do commentary.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yoric was kind enough not to make the connection explicit without my approval, which has never been provided. I'm not entirely happy that it helped inspire all this nonsense or still gets tied into it but thems the happenings.

Besides the fact that I feel no need to tie in to AWCY any time I make an artistically themed SCP it would lessen the mystery of 804 to not only confirm that it WAS intentionally created but WHY it was intentionally created. It's supposed to be a mystery about whether the thing just ended up having powers on its own, or if it was a weapon, or what.

One of my biggest problems about AWCY is that it answers all the questions about where an SCP comes from with intentional human design, which is an angle that, for me, only works when you are worried about the designs being duplicated. Worries about a new 804 being built are part of its subtext, but only part, and never something I want to confirm.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Exactly. It… rubbed me the wrong way, given the SA kids already claimed my skip is an SCP-ified, well, that thing, while I don't see any similarity.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Smapti, you can go to hell. I've been trying to come up with something similar in my meager free time and slow ways on something vaguely similar to this, but here you go writing it with more skill, attention to detail, and awesomeness than I could hope to pull off.

Bravo.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The author of that one specifically says that it's not an homage to the Fountain, but instead centers on the quote about the use of gold in the future.

The image selected just happened to be available and bears a likeness to Fountain.

If you find a really good picture of a gold toilet, he might switch it out for you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Presumably it's the same Alaska thing hinted at in the original AWCY document, which was also implied (but never stated) to be World Without Man.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My thinking is sort of that AWCY's members (in tbhis cell at least) are mostly autonomous -working on their own projects on their own schedule, occasionally collaborating with each other. The closest thing they'd have to a leader is the Critic - someone who judges their work, its execution, its message, who helps them determine whether or not they are, in fact, cool yet.

All in all, this tale is a little bit metafiction, a little bit commentary, and a little bit just trying to get into the heads of an organization that isn't pleased with its own direction.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There is no canon. This is a tale about an AWCY? group, but not an absolute in any way. We had a tale of 173 falling in love with another statue. It doesn't mean it is canon. We have a tale of 682 as an interdimensional researcher.

These are tales, and as such are the author's ideas and/or opinions. Since this is pretty well rated, I think a lot of people at least like the idea or the writing. It does sort of fit my own ideas of AWCY?. I'm not a fan of the the objects that just kill people.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
THIS

This is how you do site commentary. It directly addresses current issues while creating, referencing, and having a purpose. I love how they're a movement with a leadership that isn't really a leadership. I love "There is no manifesto". I love the continuing thread of "these damn kids don't know what they're doing anymore". Bravo, Smapti, bravo!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Though we do have an SCP "fountain" homage, and there is a manifesto-esque document. I don't take issue with your analysis of AWCY, I just feel weird about it being in character.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The very terms 'senior members / new recruits" illustrate my point.

That's actually pretty awesome — the movement is becoming what it hates.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm aware that they're saying the words, "there is no manifesto," but that reads like an excuse. If this is meant, as ChazzK suggests, to demonstrate a deterioration in AWCY, then I'd agree that it does so, but I wouldn't understand what the purpose of such a story would be. I think its telling that many of the people saying they enjoy this also state that they specifically don't like AWCY.

The very terms 'senior members / new recruits" illustrate my point. To me, it comes off as what members of the Foundation trying (badly) to mimic their best, horribly biased guess, of what AWCY might look like would act.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Remember: there is no manifesto. What's being shown here is that, if there WAS an original ideal by the folks who became the founding fathers of what is now Are We Cool Yet?, it's becoming bastardized, reclaimed, dumbed down by the newer generations. Where something once had meaning, it's now simply destruction with a label of flimsy justification put on it. Subversive art is not simply contrarianism; the most in-depth example here paints it best, "Making lolcat captions out of dead cats doesn't make people think about anything beyond 'jesus christ who would do this!?'."

It's not a condemnation or viability of the SCPs in and of themselves but rather if they "fit" as AWCY; it's pointing out that, in-character, actual members of AWCY are starting to feel that various products of their new recruits simply aren't achieving what the intended goal of the group should be. It would be like if a senior member of the GOC had to bring a young strike force to task because they were going around blowing up quantum physics laboratories, and explain to them the difference between "unknown" and "anomalous".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There is no manifesto.

In the end, though, the Critic is to an extent as closed-minded as the people whose job it is to criticize - his idea of what "real artists" do is rooted in '60s pop art and anti-establishment freakouts, and he's attacking the others for not living up to that idealogy, at the same time that he's upset with them for not evolving or pushing the envelope.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This weirdly regimented declaration of what is and isn't viable seems to me a radical 180 from everything previously established about AWCY. I can't fathom anyone who wrote, or was inspired by, The Manifesto reflecting the actions of the characters portrayed here. I get that the guy is supposed to be a 'critic', but he, and the whole cell, come off as dry, unimaginative and conditioned. It reads like a structured military organization, more akin to The Foundation itself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This fountain, not any SCP-related fountain.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You do refer to new kids who can't tell The Fountain from an urinal , though. Also, the sidewalk absorbing people was a thing of AWCY before author chopped them off.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: None of the "bad" pieces I referred to in the tale are "actual SCPs". To my knowledge, there has never been a fire hydrant that sprays gamma rays, a face-melting guitar solo, etc. The lolcats one is close to one that was later changed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really, really like the idea of (at least part of) AWCY? being led by a community college art prof codenamed The Critic. It meshes surprisingly well with my headcanon. I like that what they're doing is ALMOST a workshop (something I've been wishing to explore in an SCP but haven't yet).

I was ready to downvote this for the trashing of various (deleted) SCPs while glorifying one of your own, but you made some really, really good points. I'm impressed with this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm sure you all know about that contraption up in Alaska?

What is this referring to, since it can't be 804?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This does a very good job of explaining my own stance on the state of AWCY. Here's hoping people take it to heart.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm really sad that, at some point in the story, he didn't refer to a piece and go "It Stinks."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Kind of tacky. An in-character teardown of a bunch of actual SCPs…seems like you could have just posted in the forum instead of giving it the tale tag…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Or maybe there is a manifesto, it all depends on your take…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really don't like AWCY. I just think it's an excuse to create stupid SCPs.

However, this story makes me hate them slightly less.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Better, it still clashes of course but it's better all the same.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Cheerfully adjusted. He's now their leader "to the extent that a group such as this could have one".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
and the Critic was its leader.

Well you did call him a leader and he does act like a leader, so…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know if i'd call the Critic a "leader" in the conventional sense. He oversees the particular cell the story is based around, but his job isn't to lead as much as it is to, as the name implies, criticize. The codenames, I figure, arose out of a sort of "death of the artist" mentality - the individual producing the work is irrelevant in comparison to the work itself, so they just take on names that get passed down from person to person as people retire, get captured, etc.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah I guess it works. You've won my upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For someone who avoids most AWCY pieces, I can say I honestly enjoyed this as a story.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
the talking atom bomb

Considering that Sad Man came out of New York sometime in the '60s or '70s, I'd be surprised if Andy Warhol wasn't in some way involved with it. And if there's any real-world artist who would have been part of AWCY, Warhol is it.

But that's just my headcanon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's well written but it clashes too much with my headcanon for me to upvote, I've never seen AWCY? as having any kind of leadership let alone talent specific code-names.

"Hey Johnny, sup?"
"I told you, I'm not Johnny, I'm THE DIRECTOR!"
"The what?"
"The Director you ignorant swine"
"Uh, sure thing Director, so anyways I made that new memetic field for Bobbies project"
"That's not Bobbie, that's The Designer, christ, don't you listen to the memos?"
"We have memos?"
"Get the hell out of here already Artist"
"Who?"
"Artist, The Artist, it's your codename, sheesh"
"Can't I be Le Artiste instead?"
"No, I made the names"
"Screw you Johnny"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Author mouthpiece"! Thank you, that's the word I'm looking for.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well-written, and I agree with it, but I don't like the idea of the Anomalous Art Commission dictating the goings-on of not just AWCY, but others, like 804 and the talking atom bomb. I understand that it's a commentary, but it still gets me.

Also,

"There is no manifesto"

Thank you for that. There really isn't.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I don't see anything particularly self-inserted about the Critic, or anything analogous between any of the characters and any users. A character expressing the viewpoint of the author in a piece like this doesn't add up to a self-insert.
